back: Array: O(1) access by index, O(n) insert/delete, contiguous memory. LinkedList: O(n) access, O(1) insert/delete at known position, non-contiguous. Use array for random access, list for frequent insert/delete.

front: What is the difference between Array and LinkedList?
back: Array: O(1) access by index, O(n) insert/delete, contiguous memory. LinkedList: O(n) access, O(1) insert/delete at known position, non-contiguous. Use array for random access, list for frequent insert/delete.

front: What is the Sliding Window pattern?
back: Maintains a range [left, right] to find optimal subarrays/substrings. Fixed size: max sum of K elements. Variable size: longest substring without repeating. Time: O(n).
